message:
chore(beads): sync issue tracker state

Update issue tracker with current status of extension runtime risk
controller work, parity certification progress, and authentication
feature completion tracking.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -240,7 +240,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-1rgx","title":"Design capability prompt UX spec — information architecture and interaction model","description":"# Goal
Design the complete UX specification for capability prompts — what the user sees, what information is displayed, what actions are available, and how decisions flow through the system.

# Background
When an extension requests a sensitive capability (e.g., filesystem write, shell exec, HTTP request), the runtime needs to ask the user for permission. This task designs WHAT that interaction looks like before any code is written. Getting the UX right is critical because:
- Too much information overwhelms (users click "allow all" and defeat the purpose)
- Too little information prevents informed consent
- The prompt frequency matters — showing a dialog for every fs.readFile call is unusable

# Deliverables
1. Prompt content specification:
   - Extension identity: name, version, publisher
   - Capability requested: human-readable description (not raw enum)
   - Scope: what specifically (file path, URL, command)
   - Risk level: visual indicator (green/yellow/red)
   - Decision options: Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny / Deny Always

2. Grouping/batching rules:
   - Batch multiple requests from same capability class (e.g., 5 file reads → single prompt)
   - Never batch across risk levels (dont mix filesystem with shell exec)
   - Allow "Allow all for this session" as a convenience option

3. Decision persistence model:
   - Keyed by (extension_id, capability_type, scope_pattern)
   - Example: ("auto-commit-extension", "exec", "git *") → AllowAlways
   - Scope patterns support globs for paths and commands

4. RPC mode equivalent:
   - JSON-RPC notification format for prompts
   - Response format for decisions
   - Timeout behavior (deny after 30s with no response)

5. Mockup/wireframe for TUI widget (ASCII art acceptable)

# Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Written spec document in docs/capability-prompts.md
- [ ] Covers all 5 hostcall types: tool, exec, http, session, ui
- [ ] Includes risk classification rubric
- [ ] Includes batching algorithm pseudocode
- [ ] Includes RPC JSON schema
- [ ] Reviewed against EXTENSIONS.md spec for consistency","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"IvoryIsland","created_at":"2026-02-06T06:36:06.187098597Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:37.884885847Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:34.219000544Z","close_reason":"Completed spec doc in docs/capability-prompts.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","security","ux"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","depends_on_id":"bd-2sra","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:55Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":517,"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"DESIGN REFINEMENT (from review):

The original spec has 4 decision options: Allow Once / Allow Always / Deny / Deny Always.
After review, we need a 5th: "Allow for this session" — a middle ground between "once" and "always".

Rationale: Users commonly want to approve an extension for the current session without persisting the decision permanently. "Allow Once" is too narrow (re-prompts on every call), "Allow Always" is too broad (persists forever). "Allow for session" is the sweet spot for exploratory use.

Updated decision options:
  [Allow Once] [Allow Session] [Allow Always] [Deny] [Deny Always]

"Allow Session" decisions are stored in-memory only (cleared when session ends).

Make sure the UX spec includes this 5th option with clear visual distinction from the persistent options.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:58:37Z"},{"id":506,"issue_id":"bd-1rgx","author":"IvoryIsland","text":"Drafted full UX spec in docs/capability-prompts.md (risk rubric, batching rules + pseudocode, TUI wireframe, RPC event/command schemas) aligned with EXTENSIONS.md capability taxonomy/policy.","created_at":"2026-02-06T07:20:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rm","title":"Document procedure: add new extension to sample + rerun harness","description":"Background:

- Include checklists and commands.

Acceptance:
- A new contributor can add an extension without consulting this plan.","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss

[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseLake","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:26:29.737520573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:28:23.894006976Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T08:12:48.688333500Z","close_reason":"Documented add-extension procedure in docs/EXTENSION_SAMPLE.md","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rm","depends_on_id":"bd-29c","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:00Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-1rqs","title":"Unit tests: Extension Message & Session APIs","description":"Comprehensive unit tests for message injection and session control APIs.

## Test Categories

### pi.sendMessage() Tests
- Custom message creates correct entry type
- display=true renders in output
- display=false suppresses output
- triggerTurn=true initiates agent turn
- deliverAs modes (steer/followUp/nextTurn)

### pi.sendUserMessage() Tests
- User message triggers turn
- Content correctly formatted
- deliverAs modes work

### pi.appendEntry() Tests
- Custom entry appended to session
- Entry type preserved
- Data serialized correctly

### Session Metadata Tests
- setSessionName() persists
- getSessionName() returns current name
- setLabel() adds label to entry

### Tool Management Tests
- getActiveTools() returns enabled tools
- getAllTools() includes disabled
- setActiveTools() toggles correctly

### Model Control Tests
- setModel() changes provider/model
- getThinkingLevel() returns current
- setThinkingLevel() updates budget

### pi.events Tests
- emit() dispatches to handlers
- on() registers handler
- Handler receives correct data
- Multiple handlers for same event

## Test Approach
- Real runtime, no mocks
- VCR for any HTTP calls
- JSONL detailed logging

## Files
- tests/extensions_message_session.rs
- tests/fixtures/message_session/*.json","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:07.361517642Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:02.423999015Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:42:02.423915670Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-1i5x","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:48.313619638Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-2xgk","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:49.504233476Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-2yoh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:51.900085689Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-3lv3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:54.335445454Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-m84r","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:50.690412203Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rqs","depends_on_id":"bd-vs72","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:19:53.109410321Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
-{"id":"bd-1rrx4","title":"[PARITY-EPIC] Close all feature parity gaps: performance, auth, TUI polish, graceful degradation","description":"## Motivation

FEATURE_PARITY.md identifies 8 partial (🔶) features and 2 deferred (⬜) performance targets. This epic closes EVERY gap to achieve full feature parity between pi_agent_rust and the TypeScript Pi Agent.

**Scope note**: This epic covers completing *existing partial features* and meeting *existing performance targets*. Adding new providers (Groq, Cerebras, OpenRouter, Kimi, Qwen) is tracked separately in the PROVIDER-GAPS epic (bd-3uqg.11), which extends the feature set rather than closing existing parity gaps.

## Gap Analysis (from FEATURE_PARITY.md)

### 🔶 Partial Features:
1. **Hostcall ABI** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). dispatch_host_call_shared() routes all 7 capabilities.
2. **Extension UI bridge** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). request_ui/respond_ui cycle wired end-to-end.
3. **Extension Session API** — ACTUALLY COMPLETE (stale doc). All 12 operations dispatched.
4. **OAuth flow** — Anthropic only; generic framework exists but no built-in OpenAI/Google support.
5. **Token refresh** — Works but E2E tests missing (bd-3pn); no failure recovery UX.
6. **/login** — Only Anthropic by name; extension providers work via generic framework.
7. **/scoped-models** — Basic works; cycling verification (bd-21gp) and UI polish (bd-27a8) pending.
8. **/share** — HTML export + gist upload work via gh CLI; metadata/options deferred.

### ⬜ Deferred Performance Targets:
9. **TUI framerate** — 60fps target, currently N/A (no measurement framework).
10. **Memory (idle)** — <50MB target, not measured.

### Architectural Gaps (discovered during analysis):
11. **Full content rebuild per frame** — build_conversation_content() processes ALL messages every render.
12. **No graceful degradation** — No CPU/memory pressure detection or adaptive rendering.
13. **Memory unbounded** — No conversation virtualization or lazy loading.
14. **String allocation churn** — New String allocation per frame for conversation content.
15. **FEATURE_PARITY.md stale** — Extension features marked partial are actually complete.

## Design Philosophy

Apply alien-artifact-coding (mathematical rigor, formal guarantees, provably correct caching) and
extreme-software-optimization (profile-driven, hot-path analysis, zero-copy, allocation minimization).

## Tracks

1. **PERF** — Performance foundation: content caching, frame budgets, adaptive rendering, memory bounds (8 sub-beads after PERF-5 merged into PERF-4)
2. **AUTH** — Authentication completion: OAuth for OpenAI/Google, refresh E2E, failure recovery (5 sub-beads + research spike)
3. **TUI** — TUI polish: scoped-models, share improvements (3 sub-beads)
4. **DOC** — Documentation: update FEATURE_PARITY.md, final certification (2 sub-beads)

## Success Criteria
- All 🔶 items → ✅ Implemented
- All ⬜ Performance items → ✅ Measured and meeting targets
- Graceful degradation proven under synthetic load
- All new code covered by unit + E2E tests with JSONL logging
- FEATURE_PARITY.md reflects actual state with evidence","status":"open","priority":0,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:10:57.344118092Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T05:49:00.052440408Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1rrx4","depends_on_id":"bd-3uqg","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T05:49:00.052362312Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rrx4","depends_on_id":"bd-7zujh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:31.069563335Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rrx4","depends_on_id":"bd-cq0c3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:28.070462307Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rrx4","depends_on_id":"bd-l8pam","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:18.166831595Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-1rrx4","depends_on_id":"bd-lm7md","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:22.446583377Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-1ry","title":"Impl: WebAssembly bridge for PiJS (QuickJS has no wasm)","description":"# Assumption / Constraint
Assume **QuickJS does not provide WebAssembly** (`globalThis.WebAssembly` absent). We still must run the full pinned sample set, including extensions that ship Emscripten-style JS+WASM bundles.

# Goal
Provide a **PiWasm bridge** so JS-tier extensions can use a **WebAssembly-compatible API** even though the JS engine lacks wasm.

# Genericity constraint (non-negotiable)
- **No per-extension exceptions**: the bridge must not branch on extension id/name.
- Behavior is determined only by: wasm bytes, import object shape, policy/capabilities, and deterministic runtime configuration.
- Sample set is only used to define the **minimum required semantics**, not to justify one-off hacks.

# Strategy (hybrid)
- Use **wasmtime** (same engine as Tier A WASM host) to execute wasm.
- Expose a minimal `globalThis.WebAssembly` polyfill (or equivalent injected module) that is **generically compatible** with common JS+WASM glue patterns.
- All imports that represent side effects must route through **connector hostcalls** and therefore capabilities/logging.

# Scope / Deliverables
## WebAssembly JS surface (subset)
- `WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports)`
- `WebAssembly.instantiateStreaming(resp, imports)` (if needed; otherwise extc rewrite to non-streaming)
- `WebAssembly.compile(bytes)` / `WebAssembly.Module`
- `WebAssembly.Instance` exports behavior used by typical glue code
- `WebAssembly.Memory` with `.buffer` semantics compatible with standard glue patterns
- Traps/errors mapped to actionable Pi errors + structured logs

## Import object handling
- Support JS functions as wasm imports with deterministic call ordering.
- Restrict / gate imports that imply OS access.

## Caching + perf
- Cache compiled wasm modules keyed by sha256(bytes + wasmtime_version + policy_version).
- Minimize cross-boundary copies; measure and optimize boundary overhead.

# Security constraints
- Enforce memory/table limits per extension policy.
- Ensure wasm cannot access host OS except via explicit connector imports.
- Structured logs: every instantiate/compile includes extension_id + artifact_id + elapsed + limits.

# Acceptance (hard)
- Any **wasm-using JS-tier artifact** in the pinned sample set runs end-to-end **without manual source edits** using this bridge (or Tier A when appropriate).
- The E2E harness passes **16/16** sample extensions.
- The implementation remains **generic** (no extension-id special-casing), enforced by tests/review.

# Tests (required)
- Unit tests: wasm compile/instantiate, memory growth boundaries, import call routing, trap mapping.
- Conformance fixtures for wasm-in-js scenarios with deterministic traces.
- Security tests: denied connector imports, exceeded limits, malicious wasm.

# Dependencies
- Must compose with unified capability model (bd-2hr) and dispatcher enforcement (bd-h04).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T18:37:06.245104290Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:01.988397237Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T07:01:59.193800332Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-1f5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-1jn","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-2hr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-2ki","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-37z","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-3d1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-1ry","depends_on_id":"bd-h04","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":483,"issue_id":"bd-1ry","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Primary references for implementing the PiWasm bridge (WebAssembly-in-JS) without Node/Bun:

- wasmtime component model docs (reference for engine configuration + linking; PiWasm should share the same wasmtime policy/budgets used by the Tier-A WASM host):
  https://docs.wasmtime.dev/api/wasmtime/component/index.html

- QuickJS embedder provides the primitives PiWasm needs to stay safe + deterministic:
  - JS_SetMemoryLimit + JS_SetInterruptHandler (budgets)
  - JS_ExecutePendingJob (microtasks)
  - JS_SetModuleLoaderFunc (virtual module injection)
  Ref: https://cs.opensource.google/fuchsia/fuchsia/+/master:third_party/quickjs/quickjs.h

Design note (prove-them-wrong path):
- Zechner critique is correct for “QuickJS engine only”. PiWasm is the missing layer: Pi provides the WebAssembly API surface as a capability-gated, audited connector on top of wasmtime.
- Implementation MUST remain generic (no extension-id branching). Any special handling must be keyed only by observable bytes + manifest/policy.

Suggested build/test strategy:
- Prefer implementing WebAssembly.instantiate(bytes, imports) + WebAssembly.Memory first; add Table/Module/Instance only as demanded by the pinned sample corpus.
- Record a minimal “wasm glue” conformance fixture (imports shape + expected traps) to lock semantics before optimizing.","created_at":"2026-02-06T03:11:21Z"},{"id":738,"issue_id":"bd-1ry","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Deferred: grep of VALIDATED_MANIFEST.json and full 223-extension corpus confirms ZERO extensions use WebAssembly. PiWasm bridge is not needed for current or foreseeable extension ecosystem. Can be reopened if a WASM-using extension appears.","created_at":"2026-02-07T07:02:01Z"}]}

Surface important diagnostics after `/reload`.

# Required Diagnostics
- `models.json` parse error (if present) from `ModelRegistry::error()`.

# UX
- Present as a concise warning panel or system message.
- Include file paths and short actionable guidance.

# Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-1shy","title":"Unit tests: http client streaming + timeouts (no mocks)","description":"# Goal
Add deterministic unit/integration tests for src/http/client.rs without mocks.

# Context
HTTP client powers provider streaming, but coverage matrix shows minimal direct tests. We need real-path coverage for request building, header parsing, streaming byte delivery, and timeout behavior.

# Scope
- RequestBuilder: headers, JSON body, timeout handling.
- Response parsing: status + headers, max header bytes, chunked and content-length bodies.
- Streaming: incremental body chunks + buffered limits.
- VCR path: RecordedResponse::into_byte_stream round-trip.

# Approach
- Use a minimal local asupersync TcpListener server with canned HTTP/1.1 responses (deterministic, no mocks).
- Capture JSONL logs + artifact index via TestLogger (bd-4u9).
- Document allowlist rationale in docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md if needed.

# Acceptance Criteria
- Success + malformed header + oversized header + timeout cases covered.
- No mock providers or fake tool results.
- Tests emit JSONL logs + artifact index.

 {"id":"bd-2oz69","title":"[PERF-TEST-E2E] End-to-end performance test scripts","description":"## Scope

E2E test scripts that exercise the full performance pipeline with realistic workloads and structured logging.

### Script 1: Long Conversation Responsiveness
```
Setup: Create 500-message synthetic conversation via VCR
Action: Measure frame times during scroll-to-top, scroll-to-bottom
Assert: p95 frame time < 16ms (60fps budget)
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, frame_index, frame_time_us, cache_hit_ratio, memory_rss_bytes
```

### Script 2: Streaming With History
```
Setup: 200 messages in history, then start streaming
Action: Stream 50 tokens via VCR, measure frame times
Assert: frame time O(token_length), not O(total_conversation)
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, token_index, frame_time_us, prefix_valid, streaming_buffer_len
```

### Script 3: Degradation Under Load
```
Setup: Start TUI, inject synthetic slow frames via test hook
Action: Feed 5 consecutive >20ms frames, then 15 fast frames
Assert: Degraded mode triggered, then recovery to Normal
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, frame_index, fidelity_level, frame_time_us, consecutive_over, consecutive_under
```

### Script 4: Memory Pressure Response
```
Setup: Start TUI with mocked RSS monitor
Action: Set RSS to 150MB, verify auto-collapse; set to 250MB, verify truncation
Assert: Tool outputs collapsed, old messages truncated, session file unchanged
Logging: JSONL with fields: test_name, memory_level, rss_bytes, messages_visible, messages_truncated, tool_outputs_collapsed
```

## Test Infrastructure
- All tests use common::harness::TestHarness for JSONL logging
- Artifacts written to tests/artifacts/perf/ for CI retention
- Each test emits a summary line: PASS/FAIL with key metrics

## Dependencies
- Depends on PERF-TEST-1 through PERF-TEST-4 (integration tests should pass before E2E)

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] 4 E2E scripts with JSONL structured logging (pi.test.perf_event.v1 schema)
- [ ] Artifacts written to tests/artifacts/perf/
- [ ] p95 frame time assertions for 500-message conversation
- [ ] Streaming performance isolation verified (O(token) not O(conversation))
- [ ] Degradation + recovery cycle verified
- [ ] Memory pressure response verified (collapse + truncate)
- [ ] All tests pass

 {"id":"bd-2p71","title":"Research: npm registry scan (Pi extension packages)","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ScarletGate","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:00.092894175Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-07T02:25:22.950503385Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T02:25:22.950401696Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","npm","research"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-19rf","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:48.132355298Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:00.122709778Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-2p71","depends_on_id":"bd-kcj6","type":"related","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:07:36.765502007Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":299,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Goal
- Discover Pi extensions that are distributed primarily via npm packages (often missed by repo-only search).

Search strategy
- Use npm search + registry API to query for:
  - buildwithpi
  - pi-mono
  - pi-coding-agent
  - "Pi Agent" extension
  - extension + @mariozechner/pi-coding-agent
- Also look for extension “bundles” that ship many extensions in one package.

What to capture per candidate
- package name, version, dist tarball URL, repository URL
- weekly/monthly downloads (popularity)
- keywords/tags that indicate it is a Pi extension
- entrypoint file(s) and whether it contains ExtensionAPI import / export default

Output
- A list of npm-based candidates that can be fed into the inventory and then pinned/archived.
- For each package, include a reproducible reference (version + integrity/checksum).

Acceptance criteria
- >= 5 distinct npm queries executed and recorded.
- >= 50 plausible candidates triaged.
- >= 20 new validated “true Pi extension” entrypoints surfaced via npm.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:17:16Z"},{"id":367,"issue_id":"bd-2p71","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

npm discovery should be deterministic and replayable.

Unit tests
- Fixture-based tests for:
  - parsing npm search/registry responses
  - extracting repo URL + tarball integrity
  - detecting “true Pi extension” signatures in package contents (entrypoint scan rules)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that:
  - replays npm fixture responses
  - downloads/uses cached tarball fixtures
  - produces normalized candidate records (package@version, entrypoint paths)

Logging
- JSONL logs per run:
  - query + timestamp
  - package counts (returned/triaged/validated)
  - validation evidence (what file matched what signature)
  - perf timings (so this remains ‘super fast’)

Security
- Never log auth tokens; redact any registry headers.

 {"id":"bd-2p8","title":"Extension event hooks + cancellation semantics","description":"Background:
- EXISTING_PI_STRUCTURE defines event hooks (session_before_switch/fork/compact/etc.) that can cancel actions.
- Extension runtime must enforce ordering + cancellation and emit extension_error when handlers fail.

Scope / Steps:
1) Implement event hook dispatch order and payloads for session lifecycle + tool/turn events.
2) Honor cancellation responses (e.g., session_before_switch/fork/compact) and propagate to UI/status.
3) Add tests that verify cancellation prevents side effects and errors surface correctly.
4) Add conformance fixtures for cancellation edge cases.

Acceptance:
- Event hooks run in the correct order and can veto actions.
- Cancellation is observable in UI/RPC and session state is unchanged.
- Tests + fixtures cover both success and cancel paths.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes

-{"id":"bd-2pc62","title":"[DOC-2] Final parity certification: run all gates, generate evidence dossier","description":"## Problem

Need final verification that ALL feature parity gaps are closed with evidence.

## Solution

### Certification Checklist

1. **All tests pass**: `cargo test --all-targets` → 0 failures
2. **Clippy clean**: `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` → 0 errors
3. **Format clean**: `cargo fmt --check` → 0 changes
4. **Benchmarks pass**: `cargo bench` → all complete, no regressions
5. **Coverage above threshold**: `cargo llvm-cov` → meets CI gate
6. **FEATURE_PARITY.md audit**: Every row is ✅ or ⬜ (no 🔶 or ❌ remaining in scope)

### Evidence Dossier

Generate a JSON report:
```json
{
  "certification_date": "2026-02-XX",
  "test_results": { "passed": N, "failed": 0, "ignored": M },
  "clippy": "clean",
  "coverage": "XX%",
  "benchmarks": {
    "build_content_100msg": "X.Xms p50",
    "view_render": "X.Xms p50",
    "frame_budget_met": true
  },
  "parity_status": {
    "implemented": N,
    "partial": 0,
    "missing": 0,
    "out_of_scope": M
  }
}
```

## Files to Create
- docs/parity-certification.json (evidence report)

## Dependencies
- Depends on DOC-1 (FEATURE_PARITY.md must be current)
- Depends on all track completions

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All quality gates pass
- [ ] Evidence dossier generated
- [ ] Zero 🔶 or ❌ items remaining (in-scope)
- [ ] PARITY-EPIC can be closed","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"GentleIsland","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:10.529024462Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-14T03:31:47.109710103Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2pc62","depends_on_id":"bd-3zy1e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:20.660138183Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":1317,"issue_id":"bd-2pc62","author":"CodexGpt5","text":"Coordination note: MCP Agent Mail is unavailable (transport closed), so I’m taking a subtask here via beads.  {"id":"bd-2pzp","title":"Message queue: pending-messages UI in interactive mode","description":"# Goal

- While busy, display:
  - number of queued steering messages
  - number of queued follow-up messages
  - preview of queued text (truncate long lines)

# UX Notes
- Keep it compact; must not overwhelm the main conversation viewport.
- Prefer consistent styling (dim for follow-up vs bright for steering, etc.).

# Acceptance Criteria

 {"id":"bd-7rmt","title":"Conformance report archival and regression tracking","description":"# Conformance report archival and regression tracking

## What
Each conformance run produces a JSON report. Archive these reports and track conformance over time. Alert on regressions (pass rate drops).

## Implementation
1. After each full conformance run, save report to reports/ directory with timestamp
2. Compare current report to previous report
3. If pass rate decreased OR new failures appeared: flag regression
4. Generate trend chart: pass rate over time

## Regression Detection
- Compare extension-by-extension: any extension that was PASS and is now FAIL = regression
- Compare aggregate: overall pass rate must be >= previous run
- New extensions added (not in previous run) do not count as regressions

## Output
- reports/conformance_YYYY-MM-DD.json (archived per run)
- reports/conformance_trend.json (historical trend data)
- CI annotation on regression

## Acceptance Criteria
- Regression detected when pass rate drops
- Historical reports accessible
- Trend data shows improvement over time","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:54.722091165Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T17:57:36.185965699Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T17:57:36.185897943Z","close_reason":"Archived conformance reports + trend output + regression detection/CI annotations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7rmt","depends_on_id":"bd-19rt","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:25:54.728950744Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-7wuh","title":"Feature: Conformance + reliability evidence for expanded corpus","description":"Scale scenario suites, fixtures, determinism, and performance evidence to cover the larger extension set.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:47.352778830Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:31.963963560Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:31.593841501Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["conformance","extensions","perf","reliability"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7wuh","depends_on_id":"bd-po15","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:00:47.373355264Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":252,"issue_id":"bd-7wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Purpose
Scale conformance evidence and reliability/perf proofs to cover the expanded extension corpus.

Key Requirements
- Deterministic fixtures: network/exec interactions must be mocked or VCR-captured.
- Scenario coverage must reflect extension types (tool/command/event/provider/UI/WASM/MCP).
- Performance evidence must include load, tool-call, and event-hook overhead under realistic workloads.

Outputs
- Scenario suite templates + fixture capture guidance.
- Expanded conformance runs with structured logs and diff reports.
- Updated evidence binder and docs tying results to the selected corpus.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T06:12:53Z"},{"id":704,"issue_id":"bd-7wuh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Closed. Conformance + reliability evidence completed via bd-2fps (conformance), bd-205q (perf+reliability), bd-2jlj (full conformance epic with all 8 phases). 187/223 pass, 30 negative tests pass, reliability tests in tests/extensions_reliability.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-07T06:37:31Z"}]}
-{"id":"bd-7zujh","title":"[DOC-TRACK] Documentation: update FEATURE_PARITY.md, final parity certification","description":"## Overview

Close the loop on the parity effort:

1. **Update FEATURE_PARITY.md** — Correct stale information (extensions are complete, not partial), update test counts (263+ TUI tests), update performance measurements
2. **Final certification** — Run all tests, benchmarks, coverage; generate evidence dossier; confirm all gaps closed

## Stale Information in FEATURE_PARITY.md

- Hostcall ABI marked 🔶 but is actually ✅ (dispatch_host_call_shared routes all 7 capabilities)
- Extension UI bridge marked 🔶 but is actually ✅ (request_ui/respond_ui cycle wired end-to-end)
- Extension Session API marked 🔶 but is actually ✅ (all 12 operations dispatched)
- TUI interactive tests: "2" but actually 263+
- Test coverage total: "215" but actually much higher

## Files
- FEATURE_PARITY.md — primary document to update
- docs/TEST_COVERAGE_MATRIX.md — secondary document to update

## Success Criteria
- [ ] All 🔶 items with completed implementation corrected to ✅
- [ ] Test counts in FEATURE_PARITY.md reflect actual current values
- [ ] Performance measurements filled in from PERF-3 telemetry and PERF-8 benchmarks
- [ ] Evidence dossier generated with full test results, benchmark data, and coverage report
- [ ] DOC-1 and DOC-2 both completed and verified","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:12:03.080702796Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T04:34:57.326141967Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-7zujh","depends_on_id":"bd-2pc62","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:19.734328931Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-7zujh","depends_on_id":"bd-3zy1e","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T03:19:19.477880521Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-8mm","title":"Impl: Deterministic event loop scheduler (asupersync)","description":"# Goal
Implement the **deterministic PiJS event loop** on top of asupersync, matching the spec in `bd-123`.

# Scope / Deliverables
- Queue model: microtasks, timers, hostcall completions.
- Timer wheel or heap with stable ordering guarantees.
- Hostcall completion enqueue with stable tie-breaking.
- Single-threaded scheduler loop that is reproducible under fixed inputs.
- Trace IDs for scheduled items (for deterministic logs and replay).

# Determinism Proof Obligations
- Ordering preserved: microtasks always drain before timers.
- Tie-break: FIFO by enqueue time and sequence id.
- No hidden randomness or time-based ordering.

# Tests
- Unit tests for queue ordering and tie-breaks.
- Conformance fixtures for microtask/timer ordering.
- E2E trace replay via harness (bd-2dd).","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead (add regression coverage if applicable)
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Any new fixtures/golden outputs are deterministic and documented
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":0,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T17:22:04.581161411Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T19:24:47.985676811Z","closed_at":"2026-02-03T20:31:54.369307082Z","close_reason":"Deterministic scheduler + tests added","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8mm","depends_on_id":"bd-123","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:02Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":159,"issue_id":"bd-8mm","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Scheduler implementation complete in src/scheduler.rs. Build blocked by upstream asupersync errors (duplicate field cancel_attribution in three_lane.rs). Code compiles when asupersync is fixed. Implemented: deterministic event loop with monotone seq counter, timer heap with stable ordering, macrotask FIFO queue, cancellation support, and comprehensive unit tests (13 tests covering ordering invariants, timer behavior, and FIFO semantics).","created_at":"2026-02-03T20:29:28Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-97qh","title":"Update popularity rubric for marketplace ecosystems","notes":"Completed: executable scoring tests + E2E artifacts; fmt/check/clippy/test pass. Close blocked by parent bd-d7gn (parent-blocked).","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:41.754301235Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin","updated_at":"2026-02-06T21:26:48.486624554Z","closed_at":"2026-02-06T21:26:48.486530569Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["extensions","research","scoring"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-97qh","depends_on_id":"bd-29ko","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:20:49.776914190Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"},{"issue_id":"bd-97qh","depends_on_id":"bd-d7gn","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:41.803095724Z","created_by":"LavenderRobin"}],"comments":[{"id":303,"issue_id":"bd-97qh","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"Why
- The bd-29ko rubric is a solid baseline, but marketplace ecosystems introduce new, higher-signal popularity metrics (rank/installs/featured badges) that should be first-class.

Task
- Extend the scoring rubric so marketplace metrics can be used deterministically and audited.

Design notes
- Add a “Marketplace adoption” sub-score (or map it into Adoption/Popularity) with explicit thresholds.
- Specify how to handle missing metrics (unknown -> 0, but do not exclude by default).
- Preserve the core principle: we select for both popularity *and* coverage diversity.

Acceptance criteria
- A rubric update that explicitly defines how OpenClaw/ClawHub metrics map to the 0-100 score.
- Examples: 3 hypothetical extensions scored end-to-end using the new signals.
- No vague language; thresholds must be numeric.
","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:54Z"},{"id":371,"issue_id":"bd-97qh","author":"LavenderRobin","text":"TESTING + LOGGING REQUIREMENTS

Make the rubric *executable*.

Deliverable upgrade
- In addition to prose, define the scoring rubric as a deterministic function (inputs = popularity snapshot + capability/coverage tags; output = score breakdown + total).

Unit tests
- Add tests for:
  - marketplace metrics mapping (rank/installs -> points)
  - missing metrics handling (unknown -> explicit behavior)
  - tie-breaker rules
  - 3+ worked examples from the rubric docs (tests assert exact scores)

E2E script
- Offline E2E that runs scoring on a small fixture candidate set and produces a stable “ranked list” artifact.

Logging
- When scoring runs in bulk, emit a summary log:
  - histogram of scores
  - top-N lists per category
  - any manual override flags (should be rare and explicit)
","created_at":"2026-02-05T08:09:48Z"},{"id":586,"issue_id":"bd-97qh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Marketplace metrics are already fully implemented: EXTENSION_POPULARITY_CRITERIA.md defines OpenClaw/ClawHub rank thresholds (<=10: +6, <=50: +4, <=100: +2), featured badge (+2, cap 6), install tiers (>=10k: +5, >=2k: +4, >=500: +2, >=100: +1). extension_scoring.rs implements score_marketplace_visibility() and score_marketplace_installs() with matching thresholds. 60+ unit tests cover all tiers including marketplace. 3 worked examples in criteria doc. Missing metrics tracked as explicit null in missingSignals.","created_at":"2026-02-06T21:26:46Z"}]}
